reasoning: The question resolves YES if there is EVEN ONE DAY out of the 42-day window (May 20 to June 30) where neither the NYT 'todayspaper' summary nor the WSJ front page contains the words 'tariff' or 'tariffs'. While trade policy is a dominant news topic under the current administration, mathematical probability strongly favors an omission over a full 6-week continuous streak. Assuming even a highly inflated 95% chance of the word appearing on at least one paper's front page index every single day, the odds of a 42-day unbroken streak are only ~0.11 (0.95^42). Furthermore, WSJ does not publish a Sunday edition, meaning on the 6 Sundays during this period, the streak rests entirely on the NYT index. News cycles inevitably shift towards other crises, domestic events, weekend features, or international news that push specific trade terminology out of the summaries on slow days. Therefore, the likelihood of a single day omitting the word 'tariff' is very high.
